Implementation approach: We will build a comprehensive Laravel 10+ API system for Volopa's Out-of-Pocket Expenses with three interconnected modules. The system leverages Laravel's built-in features including Eloquent ORM, Form Request validation, API Resources, Queue jobs, and Policy-based authorization. We'll use existing Volopa infrastructure (OAuth2 middleware, FX services, file storage) and follow the platform's multi-tenant architecture with client_id scoping. The implementation follows a service-oriented approach with thin controllers, comprehensive validation, and proper transaction management. Key components include: 1) RBAC system using user_feature_permission table with delegation hierarchy, 2) CSV batch upload with synchronous validation and asynchronous processing via Laravel queues, 3) Single expense CRUD with real-time FX conversion and flexible metadata storage. We'll use Laravel's migration system for database schema, factories for testing, and API Resources for consistent response formatting. The system integrates with existing platform services including wallet currency lookup, FX conversion, and notification infrastructure.

Program call flow:
sequenceDiagram
    participant C as Client
    participant MW as Oauth2UserClient
    participant UFPC as UserFeaturePermissionController
    participant UPS as UserPermissionService
    participant PEC as PocketExpenseController
    participant PES as PocketExpenseService
    participant FXS as PocketExpenseFXService
    participant PEUC as PocketExpenseUploadController
    participant CSV as PocketExpenseCSVValidator
    participant PEJ as ProcessExpenseUpload
    participant DB as Database
    participant Queue as Queue
    
    Note over C,Queue: User Permission Management Flow
    C->>MW: POST /api/v1/user-feature-permissions
    MW->>UFPC: authenticate and authorize
    UFPC->>UPS: grantPermission(user_id, client_id, feature_id, grantor_id, manager_id)
    UPS->>DB: create UserFeaturePermission record
    DB-->>UPS: return permission
    UPS-->>UFPC: return UserFeaturePermission
    UFPC-->>C: 201 UserFeaturePermissionResource
    
    Note over C,Queue: Single Expense CRUD with FX Conversion Flow
    C->>MW: POST /api/v1/pocket-expenses
    MW->>PEC: authenticate and authorize
    PEC->>FXS: convertAmount(amount, from_currency, to_currency, date, client_id)
    FXS->>DB: query wallet base currency via join chain
    DB-->>FXS: return base currency
    FXS->>FXS: getFXRate(from_currency, to_currency, date)
    FXS->>FXS: applyCommission(rate, commission_percent)
    FXS-->>PEC: return converted amount
    PEC->>PES: createExpense(data, user_id)
    PES->>DB: begin transaction
    PES->>DB: create PocketExpense record
    PES->>DB: create PocketExpenseMetadata records
    PES->>DB: commit transaction
    DB-->>PES: return expense
    PES-->>PEC: return PocketExpense
    PEC-->>C: 201 PocketExpenseResource
    
    C->>MW: PUT /api/v1/pocket-expenses/{id}
    MW->>PEC: authenticate and authorize
    PEC->>PES: updateExpense(expense, data, user_id)
    PES->>DB: begin transaction
    PES->>DB: update PocketExpense record
    PES->>DB: update/create PocketExpenseMetadata records
    PES->>DB: commit transaction
    DB-->>PES: return updated expense
    PES-->>PEC: return PocketExpense
    PEC-->>C: 200 PocketExpenseResource
    
    C->>MW: GET /api/v1/pocket-expenses
    MW->>PEC: authenticate and authorize
    PEC->>PES: getExpensesForUser(user_id, client_id)
    PES->>DB: query expenses with metadata
    DB-->>PES: return expense collection
    PES-->>PEC: return Collection
    PEC-->>C: 200 PocketExpenseResource collection
    
    C->>MW: DELETE /api/v1/pocket-expenses/{id}
    MW->>PEC: authenticate and authorize
    PEC->>PES: deleteExpense(expense, user_id)
    PES->>DB: soft delete expense and metadata
    DB-->>PES: return success
    PES-->>PEC: return true
    PEC-->>C: 204 No Content
    
    Note over C,Queue: CSV Batch Upload with Background Processing Flow
    C->>MW: POST /api/uploads/pocket-expense/csv
    MW->>PEUC: authenticate and authorize
    PEUC->>PEUC: validate file and permissions
    PEUC->>DB: create PocketExpenseFileUpload record (status='uploaded')
    DB-->>PEUC: return upload record
    PEUC->>CSV: validate(file_path, target_user_id, client_id, admin_id)
    CSV->>CSV: preloadReferenceData(client_id)
    CSV->>CSV: validateHeaders(headers)
    CSV->>CSV: validateRow for each row
    CSV-->>PEUC: return validation result
    
    alt validation failed
        PEUC->>DB: update upload record (status='validation_failed', errors)
        DB-->>PEUC: confirm update
        PEUC-->>C: 422 validation errors
    else validation passed
        PEUC->>DB: bulk insert to PocketExpenseUploadsData (status='pending')
        DB-->>PEUC: confirm insert
        PEUC->>DB: update upload record (status='processing')
        DB-->>PEUC: confirm update
        PEUC->>Queue: dispatch ProcessExpenseUpload(upload_id)
        Queue-->>PEUC: job queued
        PEUC-->>C: 200 success response
        
        Note over PEJ: Background Processing
        Queue->>PEJ: handle()
        PEJ->>DB: fetch pending upload data in batches
        DB-->>PEJ: return expense data
        PEJ->>PES: create expenses via syncExpensesToMainService
        PES->>DB: bulk create expenses with metadata
        DB-->>PES: confirm creation
        PES-->>PEJ: return success
        PEJ->>DB: update PocketExpenseUploadsData (status='synced')
        PEJ->>DB: update PocketExpenseFileUpload (status='completed')
        DB-->>PEJ: confirm updates
    end
System constraints: All API endpoints must use Oauth2UserClient middleware applied at route group level via Route::middleware(['Oauth2UserClient'])
Controllers must NOT call $this->middleware() in constructors - Oauth2UserClient is applied at the route group level only
All queries and mutations must be scoped by client_id for multi-tenancy
user_id must match authenticated user (server-side check)
Target entities (e.g. expense_user_id) must belong to the same client_id
All tables must use InnoDB engine with utf8mb4 character set and utf8mb4_unicode_ci collation
New tables should use flag-based soft delete with deleted TINYINT(1) DEFAULT 0 and delete_time DATETIME DEFAULT NULL columns
Timestamp pattern for new tables: create_time DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP and update_time DATETIME DEFAULT NULL ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
Maximum 200 rows per CSV file upload
CSV file format must be CSV or TXT with CSV content, maximum 10MB (10240 KB)
CSV header row is mandatory and must exactly match column names
All-or-nothing validation: if any CSV row fails validation, no expenses are created
Date must not be older than 3 years from current date
Date format in CSV: DD/MM/YYYY (DD-MM-YYYY in template)
Currency Code must be 3-letter ISO and in platform allowed list
Amount sign determined by expense type (Refund = positive, others = negative)
VAT % must be numeric between 0-100 (strip % sign from input)
Source must match configured sources for client (including global Other)
Source Note required when Source = Other
Merchant Name maximum length VARCHAR(180) per database definition
Maximum 20 active expense sources per client
Unique source names per client via unique constraint (client_id, name)
Global 'Other' record (client_id = NULL) is not deletable or editable
FX rate lookup: maximum 30-day lookback from expense date
If no FX rate found, return 'No FX Available'
Backend must recalculate FX on save (do not trust frontend-only value)
Commission formula: AdjustedRate = BaseRate x (1 - Comm%)
2-second debounce before triggering FX API call in frontend
Only Primary Admin has full access to all users by default
Admin gets full access only to own expenses by default; needs explicit grant for others
Business User and Card User cannot approve expenses even with management rights
Admin can only grant access to their own managed users (not all users)
Tables with similar columns across modules are DISTINCT entities with different column types, status enums, and relationships
Do not use a class or method that doesn't exist in the current repository or was not defined in the added code
Do not add methods that already exist in the current repository
Do not return raw Eloquent models from API endpoints
Do not use session/redirect patterns in APIs
Do not return 200 status code for errors
Do not respond with inconsistent shapes/casing in API responses
Do not expose stack traces or sensitive error details in API responses
Do not disable mass-assignment protection or trust client-owned fields
Do not build query filters directly from user input without validation
Do not create N+1 queries (especially in API Resources)
Do not return unbounded lists without pagination
Do not forget transactions for multi-write operations
Keep controllers thin; push logic into model/service classes
Use transactions when touching multiple database tables
Return proper HTTP status codes: 200 (OK), 201 (Created), 204 (No Content), 400 (Bad Request), 401 (Unauthorized), 403 (Forbidden), 404 (Not Found), 422 (Unprocessable Entity)
Create API Resource to shape responses and hide internal fields
Add pagination using Resource::collection($query->paginate())
Assert JSON shape, status codes, DB state, and policy enforcement in tests
Route names should be consistent (api.v1.posts.favorite.store pattern)
Add routes to routes/api.php, usually under /v1 and OAuth2 middleware except /api/uploads/pocket-expense/csv which omits /v1 prefix per spec
Write migrations and factories for new tables
Add relationships on models with proper foreign key constraints
Create indexes/uniques/foreign keys as specified in database schema
Validate request content using Form Request classes
Use Policies or Gates for permissions and authorization
user_feature_permission table unique constraint: (user_id, client_id, feature_id)
pocket_expense_source_client_config table unique constraint: (client_id, name)
pocket_expense_metadata table unique constraint: (pocket_expense_id, metadata_type, deleted)

opt_pocket_expense_type seed data: {'option': 'ATM Withdrawal', 'amount_sign': 'negative'}, {'option': 'Point of Sale', 'amount_sign': 'negative'}, {'option': 'Fee & Charges', 'amount_sign': 'negative'}, {'option': 'Refund from Merchant', 'amount_sign': 'positive'}
pocket_expense status ENUM values: ('draft','submitted','approved','rejected') with DEFAULT 'draft'
pocket_expense_file_uploads status ENUM values: ('uploaded','validation_failed','validation_passed','processing','completed','failed','sync_failed') with DEFAULT 'uploaded'
pocket_expense_uploads_data status ENUM values: ('pending','processing','synced','failed') with DEFAULT 'pending'
pocket_expense_metadata metadata_type ENUM values: ('category','tracking_code_type_1','tracking_code_type_2','project','additional_field','file','expense_source')
Foreign key user_id references users(id) table
Foreign key client_id references clients(id) table
Foreign key feature_id references features(id) table
Foreign key expense_type references opt_pocket_expense_type(id) table
Foreign key transaction_category_id references transaction_category(id) table
Foreign key tracking_code_id references tracking_codes(id) table
Foreign key project_id references configurable_projects(id) table
Foreign key file_store_id references file_store(id) table
Foreign key expense_source_id references pocket_expense_source_client_config(id) table
Foreign key additional_field_id references expense_additional_field(id) table
CSV validation form fields: file (required|file|mimes:csv,txt|max:10240), user_id (required|exists:users,id), expense_user_id (required|exists:users,id), client_id (required|exists:clients,id)
CSV upload error response 422 structure: {success:false, message, upload_id, total_rows, error_count, errors:[{line_number, field, error, value}]}
CSV upload success response 200 structure: {success:true, message, upload_id, total_rows}
3 default expense sources auto-created on feature enable: Cash, Corporate Card, Personal Card
File storage path pattern: storage/app/pocket-expense-uploads
Background job queue name: expense-processing
FX conversion integration via /wallet-ccy-value endpoint (existing)
Queue job ProcessExpenseUpload syncs to main service in batches of 100
CSV column Date maps to pocket_expense.date with format dd-mm-yyyy validation
CSV column Expense Type maps to opt_pocket_expense_type.option with dropdown validation
CSV column Currency Code maps to pocket_expense.currency with 3-letter ISO validation
CSV column Amount maps to pocket_expense.amount with DECIMAL(14,2) format
CSV column VAT % maps to pocket_expense.vat_amount with 0-100 numeric validation
CSV column Merchant Name maps to pocket_expense.merchant_name with VARCHAR(180) limit
CSV column Description maps to pocket_expense.merchant_description
CSV column Merchant Address maps to pocket_expense.merchant_address
CSV column Source maps to pocket_expense_source_client_config.name with client source validation
CSV column Source Note maps to pocket_expense_metadata with required when Source = Other
CSV column Notes maps to pocket_expense.notes with DB limit and SQL injection prevention
System fields not in CSV: user_id (from selected user), status (default 'submitted'), created_by_user_id (logged-in user), timestamps and audit fields
ProcessExpenseUpload job updates upload status to 'completed' and sets processed_at timestamp on completion
Database column pocket_expense.user_id maps to API form field expense_user_id (target user)
Database column pocket_expense_file_uploads.created_by_user_id maps to API form field user_id (admin)
All new Models must extend Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model
All new Controllers must extend App\Http\Controllers\Controller
All new Services must be plain PHP classes with dependency injection
All new Policies must extend Illuminate\Auth\Access\HandlesAuthorization
All new Form Requests must extend Illuminate\Foundation\Http\FormRequest
All new Resources must extend Illuminate\Http\Resources\Json\JsonResource
All new Jobs must implement Illuminate\Contracts\Queue\ShouldQueue
All new Factories must extend Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Factories\Factory
Anything UNCLEAR: Two key areas require clarification based on the unresolved decisions in the YAML context: 1) The specific notification channels for batch upload completion (DEC-UNRESOLVED-001) - whether to use in-app notifications, email, push notifications, or a combination. This affects the implementation of the notification system in the ProcessExpenseUpload job. 2) The exact implementation details for the ClientFeatures model and how to check if a client has the OOP Expense feature enabled (DEC-UNRESOLVED-004) - specifically the backing table structure and the query pattern to verify feature_id = 16 is enabled for a client. Additionally, the audit logging mechanism for permission grants/revokes needs definition (DEC-UNRESOLVED-003) - whether to implement a project-specific audit table or integrate with existing platform audit infrastructure. The soft delete convention consistency (ENV-DEC-UNRESOLVED-003) also needs resolution - whether new tables should use flag-based (deleted + delete_time) or Laravel SoftDeletes (deleted_at) pattern.
